title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The open-source vulnerability scanner itself does not pose a direct threat but could be exploited if malicious actors modify or distribute a compromised version. Additionally, over-reliance on such tools without proper validation may lead to false positives or negatives, potentially impacting security workflows.
recommended_actions: Evaluate the tool's source code and verify its integrity before use.
Implement the tool in a controlled environment and cross-validate its findings with other trusted vulnerability scanners.
Educate teams on the limitations of automated tools and emphasize the importance of manual review for critical systems.

title: A denial-of-service vulnerability exists in NPort devices because of improper access control on...
description: A denial-of-service vulnerability exists in NPort devices because of improper access control on the command port. The command interface does not properly validate whether a sender is associated with a valid data port session before accepting break signal commands. A remote attacker with network access can send crafted requests to disrupt serial communication for an active user session.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: This vulnerability allows a remote attacker to disrupt serial communication for active user sessions on NPort devices, potentially leading to operational downtime and disruption of critical services relying on serial communication.
recommended_actions: Apply the latest firmware update or patch provided by the device manufacturer to address the vulnerability.
Implement network segmentation and restrict access to the command port using firewalls or access control lists (ACLs) to limit exposure to trusted IP addresses only.
Monitor network traffic for unusual activity targeting NPort devices and enable logging to detect potential exploitation attempts.

title: A command injection vulnerability was found in galaxy_ng. The do_git_checkout() function in the...
description: A command injection vulnerability was found in galaxy_ng. The do_git_checkout() function in the legacy role import API (v1) interpolates unsanitized git ref names (branch/tag names) into shell commands executed via subprocess.run() with shell=True. An authenticated user who controls a git repository can create a branch or tag with shell metacharacters in the name to achieve remote code execution on the pulp worker. The vulnerable endpoint is only reachable when GALAXY_ENABLE_LEGACY_ROLES is set

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-12398.

title: subsys/net/ip/icmpv6.c reads the network interface from a net_pkt after that packet has been...
description: subsys/net/ip/icmpv6.c reads the network interface from a net_pkt after that packet has been handed to net_try_send_data(). In icmpv6_handle_echo_request() and net_icmpv6_send_error(), the post-send statistics update calls net_pkt_iface(reply)/net_pkt_iface(pkt) on the just-sent packet. The send path (net_try_send_data - net_if_tx) unreferences and may free the packet back to its memory slab before returning — synchronously in the RX thread when no TX queue is configured (CONFIG_NET_TC_TX_COUNT

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-10638.

title: An improper authentication security issue exists within the 1794-AENTR adapter's embedded web...
description: An improper authentication security issue exists within the 1794-AENTR adapter's embedded web server. The vulnerability allows an unauthenticated attacker to change the device's web interface password by sending a crafted HTTP GET request to a specific endpoint, without any prior authentication being required. If exploited, this could lead to unauthorized access, account takeover, and loss of the device’s embedded web server’s availability.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows unauthenticated attackers to take over the device's web interface by changing the password, potentially leading to unauthorized access, disruption of operations, and loss of control over critical systems.
recommended_actions: Apply the latest firmware update or patch provided by the vendor to address CVE-2026-0647.
Implement network segmentation to isolate vulnerable devices from external access and restrict access to trusted IPs only.
Monitor network traffic for unusual activity targeting the device's web server and configure intrusion detection/prevention systems to block malicious requests.

title:
An authentication
bypass security issue exists within FactoryTalk Historian Site Edition. By...
description:
An authentication
bypass security issue exists within FactoryTalk Historian Site Edition. By
continually sending requests to the login endpoint, an attacker may obtain a
valid authentication token.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The authentication bypass vulnerability in FactoryTalk Historian Site Edition allows attackers to potentially gain unauthorized access to sensitive systems and data by obtaining valid authentication tokens. This could lead to data breaches, system manipulation, or operational disruptions.
recommended_actions: Apply the latest security patch or update from the vendor addressing CVE-2025-13036 immediately.
Implement rate limiting and monitoring on the login endpoint to detect and block repeated unauthorized requests.
Conduct a comprehensive review of authentication mechanisms and enable multi-factor authentication (MFA) where possible.

title:
A denial of service security issue exists in the
affected product. The security issue stems from...
description:
A denial of service security issue exists in the
affected product. The security issue stems from a fault occurring when a
crafted CIP message is sent. Devices with less memory are more likely to be
affected. This can result in a major nonrecoverable fault (MNRF). A program
download is required to recover.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-11317.

title: A denial-of-service security issue exists within the 1794-AENTR adapter due to improper memory...
description: A denial-of-service security issue exists within the 1794-AENTR adapter due to improper memory handling of CIP protocol requests. This vulnerability can result in the adapter faulting and losing connection to its associated I/O modules, requiring a manual reset to recover.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-0646.

title: A sensitive information disclosure security issue exists within the affected CompactLogix...
description: A sensitive information disclosure security issue exists within the affected CompactLogix controllers. The controller's web server exposes CIP Connection IDs on the diagnostics webpage, which are accessible to any unauthenticated user on the network. This information can be leveraged by an attacker to construct malicious packets, leading to Denial-of-Service.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-9307.

title: A security issue was identified in Pavilion due to improper authorization enforcement in API...
description: A security issue was identified in Pavilion due to improper authorization enforcement in API endpoints. This vulnerability can allow an unauthorized actor to execute privileged operations, including user/role management and other administrative actions.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Pavilion's API endpoints due to improper authorization enforcement can enable unauthorized actors to perform privileged operations, such as user and role management, potentially leading to data breaches, system compromise, and loss of administrative control.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply patches or updates provided by the vendor to address CVE-2025-14272.
Implement strict access controls and monitor API activity for signs of unauthorized access or privilege escalation.
Conduct a security audit of API endpoints to ensure proper authorization enforcement and identify other potential vulnerabilities.

title: Dell Peripheral Manager, versions prior to 1.7.3, contain an uncontrolled search path element...
description: Dell Peripheral Manager, versions prior to 1.7.3, contain an uncontrolled search path element vulnerability. An attacker could potentially exploit this vulnerability through preloading malicious dll., leading to arbitrary code execution.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2024-22447.

title: Memory safety bugs present in Firefox ESR 115.36, Firefox ESR 140.11, Thunderbird ESR 140.11,...
description: Memory safety bugs present in Firefox ESR 115.36, Firefox ESR 140.11, Thunderbird ESR 140.11, Firefox 151 and Thunderbird 151. Some of these bugs showed evidence of memory corruption and we presume that with enough effort some of these could have been exploited to run arbitrary code. This vulnerability was fixed in Firefox 152, Firefox ESR 140.12, and Firefox ESR 115.37.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Memory safety bugs in Firefox and Thunderbird could allow attackers to exploit memory corruption vulnerabilities, potentially leading to arbitrary code execution. This poses a significant risk to systems using these versions, especially if they are unpatched.
recommended_actions: Immediately update to the patched versions: Firefox 152, Firefox ESR 140.12, and Firefox ESR 115.37.
Conduct a vulnerability scan across all systems to identify instances of affected software versions.
Implement monitoring and intrusion detection systems to identify any suspicious activity that may indicate exploitation attempts.

title: A security issue exists within 1769 CompactLogix controllers due to the missing validation of...
description: A security issue exists within 1769 CompactLogix controllers due to the missing validation of sequence numbers and source IP addresses in the CIP protocol. This allows attacker to abuse the exposed Connection ID’s visible on the web interface to perform denial-of-service attacks, resulting in a minor fault.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2025-11694.

title: Firefox for iOS used partial domain matching when attaching cookies to PDF requests, allowing a...
description: Firefox for iOS used partial domain matching when attaching cookies to PDF requests, allowing a malicious site on a suffix domain to receive cookies belonging to the target site. This vulnerability was fixed in Firefox for iOS 152.0.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-53899.

title: Incorrect boundary conditions in the Internationalization component. This vulnerability was fixed...
description: Incorrect boundary conditions in the Internationalization component. This vulnerability was fixed in Firefox ESR 140.12 and Firefox ESR 115.37.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the Internationalization component could allow attackers to exploit incorrect boundary conditions, potentially leading to data corruption, crashes, or arbitrary code execution. This could compromise the integrity and availability of affected systems.
recommended_actions: Immediately update Firefox ESR to version 140.12 or 115.37, as these versions contain the necessary security fix.
Conduct a vulnerability scan to identify systems running outdated versions of Firefox ESR and prioritize their updates.
Monitor systems for any unusual activity or signs of exploitation related to this vulnerability.

title: A session fixation vulnerability has been identified in osTicket v1.18.2. This security flaw...
description:
A session fixation vulnerability has been identified in osTicket v1.18.2. This security flaw allows an attacker to hijack a victim’s account by keeping the initial session identifier (OSTSESSID) active after a successful login.



The issue lies in the fact that the application does not invalidate the pre-authentication cookie or generate a new identifier for the authenticated context. As a result, if an attacker manages to set a known session identifier in the victim’s browser, they will be abl

description: Memory safety bug fixed in Firefox ESR 140.12. This vulnerability was fixed in Firefox ESR 140.12.

title: Incorrect boundary conditions in the Graphics: CanvasWebGL component. This vulnerability was...
description: Incorrect boundary conditions in the Graphics: CanvasWebGL component. This vulnerability was fixed in Firefox 152 and Firefox ESR 140.12.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the CanvasWebGL component could allow attackers to exploit incorrect boundary conditions, potentially leading to memory corruption, denial of service, or remote code execution. title: CVE-2026-11642 - Use after free in Web Apps in Google Chrome prior to 149.0.7827.103 allowed a remote attacker who ha
description: Use after free in Web Apps in Google Chrome prior to 149.0.7827.103 allowed a remote attacker who had compromised the renderer process to potentially perform a sandbox escape via a crafted HTML page. (Chromium security severity: Critical)

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: This vulnerability allows a remote attacker to escape the Chrome sandbox by exploiting a use-after-free flaw in Web Apps. Successful exploitation could lead to arbitrary code execution, potentially compromising the entire system.
recommended_actions: Immediately update Google Chrome to version 149.0.7827.103 or later to mitigate the vulnerability.
Enable automatic updates for Chrome to ensure future vulnerabilities are patched promptly.
Monitor systems for unusual activity and review logs for signs of exploitation, particularly in environments where Chrome is heavily used.
